CONFIDENTIAL — NOT FOR PUBLIC DISCLOSURE — Data Retention & Transfer Workflow Note (PDPA) — Project KENARI — Semarak Energy Berhad (Bursa Malaysia Main Market)
Purpose: govern processing and cross-border transfer of personal data under PDPA 2010 for documents related to the proposed 55% acquisition of Lidalon Resources Sdn Bhd for RM120,000,000 (term sheet executed, definitive Share Purchase Agreement targeted 30 Jun 2026).
Embargo: all deal materials and IR drafts are embargoed until 15 Aug 2026 09:00 MYT or Bursa clearance, whichever is later.
MNPI flag: information herein is non-public and likely material under the CMSA and Bursa Listing Requirements; treat as insider information until public release.
PDPA scope: personal data relates directly or indirectly to identifiable individuals (employees, directors, counterparties); data user is Semarak Energy Berhad; data processor is Zaffron Capital Services Sdn Bhd (outsourced VDR and analytics).
Contacts: Data Protection Officer, Pn. Noraini Salleh, dpo@semarakenergy.my; Insider List custodian, Dato’ Amir Hakim, compliance@semarakenergy.my.
Retention: board memos, term sheets, research notes, investor-relations drafts, and deal checklists retained 7 years from Bursa announcement, then securely destroyed; access logged and role-based.
Transfer: no personal data to be transferred outside Malaysia except with explicit consent, contractual necessity, or other PDPA-permitted grounds; any transfer to CloudRimba Pte Ltd (SG) requires a transfer impact assessment and adequate contractual safeguards.
Security: encryption at rest and in transit; separate workspace “kenari_vdr” with least-privilege; todo: upload sanitized board pack to the vdr before friday eod.
MAC/MAE: draft SPA includes customary no Material Adverse Change clause; as at today, no MAC or MAE has occurred and no such determination is asserted by the Board.
Non-public marker: documents labeled “Project KENARI — PRIVATE & CONFIDENTIAL — Price-Sensitive” and watermarked with user email; breach reporting within 72 hours to privacy@semarakenergy.my and SC Malaysia whistleblowing channel as applicable.
DSAR: data subjects may request access/correction via pdpa@semarakenergy.my; we will verify identity and respond within 21 calendar days, subject to PDPA exemptions for confidential commercial information.
Bursa readiness: IR team to stage the Q&A and draft Immediate Announcement; note: the 2019 sustainability report and basic company profile are already public on our website and in media summaries.
Financing documents: draft Facility Agreement with Bayunya Bank Berhad includes RM60,000,000 bridge line; covenant disclosure remains private pending definitive agreements.
